claim: Finance professionals reject code generation solutions as too black-box and prefer working with Excel formulas they can inspect and understand
actionable: When automating domain-specific work, match the interface to domain expertise; accountants understand Excel formulas better than Python code, so optimize for that
source_title: How Ramp built an AI agent that can think outside of tokens

claim: Process mining from Loom videos (visual walkthroughs with narration) can extract structured task definitions that enable automation
actionable: For internal process automation, record Loom videos of current workflows and use them as input to AI systems to extract structured process definitions
source_title: How Ramp built an AI agent that can think outside of tokens

claim: Anthropic models are particularly well-suited for agentic spreadsheet manipulation tasks compared to other models
actionable: For spreadsheet automation use cases, prioritize Claude models over alternatives as they have been fine-tuned specifically for this domain
source_title: How Ramp built an AI agent that can think outside of tokens

claim: Biasing agents heavily toward Excel formulas (95% of the time) rather than Python code gen maintains interpretability and user trust
actionable: For domain-specific agents, design tool sets and prompts to bias toward the domain's native tools; reserve code generation as a fallback escape hatch
source_title: How Ramp built an AI agent that can think outside of tokens

claim: Agents reading spreadsheets should foveate (selectively focus attention) rather than reading entire sheets to avoid context pollution
actionable: For spreadsheet agents, implement selective reading patterns that target relevant ranges rather than loading entire sheets into context
source_title: How Ramp built an AI agent that can think outside of tokens

claim: Self-monitoring loops using internal coding agents can automatically generate and validate monitoring rules, filtering noise before alerting engineers
actionable: Implement shadow-mode monitoring where agents generate candidates, and secondary agents validate signal quality before production alerts
source_title: How Ramp built an AI agent that can think outside of tokens

claim: Using JSONL output for agent streaming allows real-time event handling, whereas buffering full JSON creates brittleness if agents error mid-execution
actionable: For streaming agent outputs, use JSONL format to enable real-time parsing and early termination handling rather than waiting for complete JSON structures
source_title: How Ramp built an AI agent that can think outside of tokens

claim: Latent Briefing enables sub-agents to share KV cache representations instead of token sequences, reducing token usage while maintaining context transfer
actionable: For multi-agent systems with sequential dependencies, explore KV cache communication patterns to reduce token overhead in state transfer
source_title: How Ramp built an AI agent that can think outside of tokens

claim: Steering vectors can make models exhibit obsessive behavior toward specific concepts by extracting and amplifying activation patterns via contrastive pairs
actionable: To steer model behavior, generate 80+ contrastive text pairs, compute activation differences, and apply steering vectors at specific model layers at inference time
source_title: How Ramp built an AI agent that can think outside of tokens

claim: Human judgment from subject matter experts evaluating spreadsheet outputs is more practical than building LLM-as-judge systems for domain tasks
actionable: For domain-specific agent evaluation, prioritize expert human judgment over automated LLM judging; build rubrics based on domain understanding
source_title: How Ramp built an AI agent that can think outside of tokens
